diagrams.title: K5 - Complete Graph on 5 Vertices
diagrams.description: A complete graph where every pair of 5 vertices is connected by an edge. This is one of the two fundamental non-planar graphs.
elements: Every triangle is a face in 3D representation
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why K5 is non-planar: has 10 edges exceeding 3(5)-6=9 limit, and contains itself as subdivision.

diagrams.title: K3,3 - Complete Bipartite Graph
diagrams.description: Two sets of 3 vertices with all possible connections between sets. The utility graph - cannot be drawn without crossings.
elements: 9 edges connecting every A to every B
diagrams.purpose: Learning the second fundamental non-planar graph in Kuratowski's theorem.

diagrams.title: Euler's Formula Visualization
diagrams.description: A cube demonstrating v=8, e=12, f=6. Shows v-e+f = 8-12+6 = 2.
diagrams.purpose: Visual proof that Euler's formula holds for connected planar graphs.

diagrams.title: Graph Isomorphism Example
diagrams.description: Two differently labeled graphs that are structurally identical - demonstrating isomorphism preserves structure not labels.
elements: Graph G: vertices A,B,C,D with edges AB,BC,CD,DA
Mapping f(A)=1, f(B)=2, f(C)=3, f(D)=4 preserves adjacency
diagrams.purpose: Understanding that isomorphism is about structure, not vertex names.
